input.prompt:
I need to understand the splitzy codebase to plan updating link preview meta tags (Open Graph) for receipt pages at URL `/receipts/:id`.

Currently when pasting the production URL into Slack/Discord, the preview shows the default CRA title "Splitzy" and description "Web site created using create-react-app".

Please investigate and report:

1. **Frontend stack**: Is this Create React App, Vite, Next.js, or something else? Where is `index.html` located? Show its current `<head>` (title, meta description, OG tags).
2. **Receipt page route**: Find the route handler/component for `/receipts/:id`. What component renders it? What data (merchant, date) is available client-side and via what API endpoint?
3. **Backend**: Is there a Python/Flask backend (I see references to `backend/` and Flask-Migrate)? Does it serve the SPA's index.html, and could it inject per-route meta tags? Find where the SPA index.html is served from on the backend (any catch-all route).
4. **Receipt data shape**: What fields does a receipt have (merchant name, date, total)? Find the model/schema.
5. **Any existing meta-tag handling**: Search for `react-helmet`, `og:`, `meta name="description"`, or similar.
6. **Hosting/deploy**: Any clues about how this is deployed in production (vercel, render, fly, etc.)? Check package.json, render.yaml, vercel.json, fly.toml, Dockerfile.

Report back with file paths and key snippets. Keep under 400 words.

content.text:
Based on my investigation, here's the complete codebase analysis:

## **Report: Splitzy Open Graph Meta Tags Investigation**

### **1. Frontend Stack: Vite-based React SPA**
- **Framework**: Not Create React App—Vite with React 19.0.0 and React Router v7.9.6
- **Frontend location**: `/Users/s4lly/dev/splitzy/frontend/`
- **index.html**: `/Users/s4lly/dev/splitzy/frontend/index.html`
- **Current `<head>` tags** (lines 1-27):
  - `<title>Splitzy</title>`
  - `<meta name="description" content="Web site created using create-react-app">` ← Generic CRA placeholder
  - No Open Graph tags present
  - No dynamic meta tag injection library (no react-helmet, next-seo, etc.)

### **2. Receipt Page Route Handler**
- **Route definitions**: `/Users/s4lly/dev/splitzy/frontend/src/App.tsx` (lines 50, 53, 55)
  - `/receipts/:receiptId` → `<ReceiptRoute />`
  - `/receipt/:receiptId` → `<ReceiptRoute />` (alternate)
- **Component chain**: `ReceiptRoute` → `ReceiptCollabPage` (at `/Users/s4lly/dev/splitzy/frontend/src/pages/ReceiptCollabPage.tsx`)
- **Data access**: Uses `useQuery(queries.receipt.byId(...))` via Rocicorp Zero client-side query
  - Parses `receiptId` from URL params (line 25)
  - Receipt data available after query succeeds, but **only client-side**

### **3. Backend: Flask Monolith Serving API Only**
- **Location**: `/Users/s4lly/dev/splitzy/backend/`
- **Entry point**: `/Users/s4lly/dev/splitzy/backend/app.py` and `/Users/s4lly/dev/splitzy/backend/__init__.py`
- **Key finding**: Backend is **API-only** (no static file serving, no SPA index.html catch-all, no SSR)
  - Blueprints registered: `webhooks`, `receipts`
  - Routes are `/api/analyze-receipt`, `/api/health`
  - No `app.route()`, `send_static_file()`, or catch-all route for serving `index.html`
  - **Backend cannot inject meta tags into the SPA**

### **4. Receipt Data Shape** 
- **Model**: `/Users/s4lly/dev/splitzy/backend/models/user_receipt.py`
- **Available fields**:
  - `merchant` (Text, nullable) - merchant name
  - `date` (Date, nullable) - transaction date
  - `total` (Numeric(12,2), nullable) - final amount
  - `image_path` (URL to receipt image)
  - Plus 20+ other fields (tax, tip, subtotal, line items, etc.)

### **5. Existing Meta Tag Handling: None**
- Zero references to `react-helmet`, `og:`, or meta tag libraries
- PostHog analytics integrated (`posthog-js`) but no OG tag strategy
- All SPA routing happens client-side; no server can intercept and rewrite headers

### **6. Hosting/Deployment**
- **Frontend**: Deployed on **Vercel** (via `frontend/vercel.json` lines 1-19)
  - Vite framework specified
  - Rewrites API calls to `https://splitzy-prod.onrender.com/api/:path*`
  - Catch-all: `/(.*) → /index.html` (client-side routing fallback)
- **Backend**: Deployed on **Render** (`splitzy-prod.onrender.com`)
  - Flask app with PostgreSQL (Neon) in production, SQLite fallback for dev
  - Dockerfile exists for containerization
- **Zero Replica**: Separate Rocicorp Zero cache instance (Docker image `rocicorp/zero:0.25.12`)

---

**Critical blocker**: The SPA is fully client-rendered with **no SSR, no backend serving static files, and no server-side meta tag injection point**. To solve the link preview problem, you'd need:
1. A backend catch-all for `/receipts/:id` that pre-fetches receipt data and injects OG tags into the HTML, OR
2. A Next.js migration with API routes and SSR, OR
3. A separate meta tag service (e.g., og.dev) that fetches receipt data at preview time.

content.text: I have enough to draft a plan, but two things shape the design — let me ask before finalizing.

questions.question: Are receipt detail pages publicly viewable, or do they require login?

options.label: Public — anyone with the link can view
options.description: Slack/Discord crawlers can fetch receipt data anonymously. OG previews will work straightforwardly.

options.label: Auth required
options.description: Crawlers can't fetch receipt data. We'll need a separate public endpoint that returns only safe preview fields (merchant, date) for use in OG generation.

questions.question: What should the OG description include alongside merchant + date?

options.label: Merchant + date + total
options.description: e.g. title: 'Trader Joe's — Apr 12, 2026', description: 'Receipt total $48.21 · Split with friends on Splitzy'
options.preview:
Title:  Trader Joe's — Apr 12, 2026
Desc:   Receipt total $48.21 · Split with friends on Splitzy

options.label: Merchant + date only
options.description: Keep total off the preview (e.g. for privacy).
options.preview:
Title:  Trader Joe's — Apr 12, 2026
Desc:   View and split this receipt on Splitzy

options.label: Merchant + date + item count
options.description: Surface number of line items instead of total.
options.preview:
Title:  Trader Joe's — Apr 12, 2026
Desc:   12 items · Split with friends on Splitzy

questions.question: Where should the OG tag injection live?

options.label: Vercel serverless function rewrite (Recommended)
options.description: Add a Vercel function that handles /receipts/:id, fetches receipt from Render API, and serves index.html with injected meta tags. Other routes unchanged. No backend or framework changes.
options.preview:
frontend/api/og-receipt.ts  (new)
frontend/vercel.json         (rewrite /receipts/:id → /api/og-receipt)
frontend/index.html          (also fix default title/description)

options.label: Backend Flask catch-all serving SPA
options.description: Move SPA hosting onto Flask so it can inject per-route meta tags. Larger architectural change.

options.label: Just fix static defaults in index.html
options.description: Replace the CRA placeholder with generic Splitzy tags. No per-receipt metadata — every receipt link looks the same.

# Plan: Per-receipt Open Graph link previews

## Context

When a Splitzy production URL is pasted into Slack, Discord, iMessage, etc., the unfurled card shows the leftover Create-React-App boilerplate:

> Splitzy
> Web site created using create-react-app

The user wants `/receipts/:id` links to unfurl with meaningful info — at minimum the merchant and date, plus total. The non-receipt routes can keep generic-but-correct Splitzy branding.

The frontend is a Vite SPA with client-side routing only — link-preview crawlers (Slackbot, Discordbot, Twitterbot) don't run JS, so anything React renders is invisible to them. The fix has to inject meta tags server-side before the HTML reaches the crawler. Receipts are publicly viewable, so this is straightforward.

## Approach

A Vercel serverless function intercepts `/receipts/:id` (and `/receipt/:id`), fetches a small public preview payload from the backend, and returns the SPA's `index.html` with OG/Twitter meta tags injected into `<head>`. All other routes continue to serve `index.html` unchanged via the existing rewrite. The same function path also fixes the static defaults so any non-receipt unfurl shows real Splitzy branding rather than the CRA placeholder.

This requires one new public backend endpoint that returns just the fields safe to expose in a preview (merchant, date, total) — keeping the OG function fast and avoiding leaking line items / assignments / user data.

## Files to change

### Backend

1. **`backend/blueprints/receipts.py`** — add a new public route:
   - `GET /api/receipts/<int:receipt_id>/preview`
   - No auth (matches "public link" model). Returns `{ merchant, date (ISO), total }` or 404.
   - Use existing `UserReceipt` model (`backend/models/user_receipt.py`); reuse the schema field selection pattern from `backend/schemas/receipt.py` rather than hand-serializing.
   - Set `Cache-Control: public, max-age=300, s-maxage=3600` so crawlers and Vercel both cache.
   - Soft-deleted receipts return 404 (check `deleted_at` if present).

2. **`backend/tests/test_receipts.py`** — add tests:
   - 200 with expected fields for an existing receipt.
   - 404 for unknown id and soft-deleted receipts.
   - No auth header required.

### Frontend / Vercel

3. **`frontend/api/og-receipt.ts`** *(new)* — Vercel serverless function:
   - Parse receipt id from `req.url` (handles both `/receipts/:id` and `/receipt/:id`; ignore trailing slashes / query strings).
   - `fetch('https://splitzy-prod.onrender.com/api/receipts/<id>/preview')` with a short timeout (~1.5s) and `AbortController`.
   - Read `frontend/dist/index.html` from the deployed bundle (Vercel includes it; use `fs.readFileSync` against `process.cwd()` or import via `@vercel/edge`'s asset support — node runtime is simpler here).
   - Inject the meta-tag block (template below) by replacing a sentinel placeholder we add to `index.html` (e.g. `<!--OG_TAGS-->`) — safer than regex on `<head>`.
   - On any failure (timeout, 404, fetch error) fall back to the default tag block so the page still loads with generic branding.
   - HTML-escape merchant text before injection.
   - Respond with `Content-Type: text/html` and a sensible `Cache-Control` (e.g. `public, s-maxage=3600, stale-while-revalidate=86400`).

4. **`frontend/vercel.json`** — add rewrites *before* the catch-all:
   ```json
   { "source": "/receipts/:id", "destination": "/api/og-receipt?id=:id" },
   { "source": "/receipt/:id",  "destination": "/api/og-receipt?id=:id" }
   ```
   Keep the existing `/api/:path*` proxy and `/(.*) → /index.html` fallback below these.

5. **`frontend/index.html`** — replace the CRA-era defaults and add the placeholder:
   - Replace `<title>Splitzy</title>` and the `description` meta with real defaults.
   - Add default OG + Twitter card tags (title, description, type=website, site_name, image).
   - Add `<!--OG_TAGS-->` sentinel inside `<head>` so the function can swap it.
   - Remove the stale CRA comment block.

## Meta-tag template (rendered by `og-receipt.ts`)

For a receipt with `merchant="Trader Joe's"`, `date="2026-04-12"`, `total=48.21`:

```html
<title>Trader Joe's — Apr 12, 2026 · Splitzy</title>
<meta name="description" content="Receipt total $48.21 · Split with friends on Splitzy" />

<meta property="og:type" content="website" />
<meta property="og:site_name" content="Splitzy" />
<meta property="og:title" content="Trader Joe's — Apr 12, 2026" />
<meta property="og:description" content="Receipt total $48.21 · Split with friends on Splitzy" />
<meta property="og:url" content="https://<host>/receipts/80" />
<meta property="og:image" content="https://<host>/logo512.png" />

<meta name="twitter:card" content="summary" />
<meta name="twitter:title" content="Trader Joe's — Apr 12, 2026" />
<meta name="twitter:description" content="Receipt total $48.21 · Split with friends on Splitzy" />
<meta name="twitter:image" content="https://<host>/logo512.png" />
```

Edge cases:
- Missing merchant → use `"Receipt"` as the noun: `Receipt — Apr 12, 2026`.
- Missing date → drop the date segment entirely.
- Missing total → description becomes `"Split this receipt with friends on Splitzy"`.
- Currency: backend stores `Numeric(12,2)` without currency code; format as `$X.XX` for now (matches existing UI assumption).

## Verification

1. **Backend**: `cd backend && source .venv/bin/activate && pytest tests/test_receipts.py -k preview` — new tests pass.
2. **Backend smoke**: `curl https://splitzy-prod.onrender.com/api/receipts/80/preview` after deploy returns expected JSON.
3. **Frontend local**: `vercel dev` from `frontend/`, then `curl -A "Slackbot" http://localhost:3000/receipts/80` — confirm the response HTML contains the merchant/date/total in the title and OG tags.
4. **Production unfurl**: After deploy, paste `https://<prod-domain>/receipts/80` into Slack and Discord; verify the card shows merchant + date in the title and total in the description. Also test:
   - A non-receipt URL (e.g. `/`) — should show the new generic Splitzy defaults, not CRA boilerplate.
   - An invalid receipt id — should fall back to defaults, not 500.
5. **Debug helpers**: Slack's link debugger and `https://www.opengraph.xyz/url/<url>` for inspecting tags without re-pasting.

# Plan: Per-receipt Open Graph link previews

## Context

When a Splitzy production URL is pasted into Slack, Discord, iMessage, etc., the unfurled card shows the leftover Create-React-App boilerplate:

> Splitzy
> Web site created using create-react-app

The user wants `/receipts/:id` links to unfurl with meaningful info — at minimum the merchant and date, plus total. The non-receipt routes can keep generic-but-correct Splitzy branding.

The frontend is a Vite SPA with client-side routing only — link-preview crawlers (Slackbot, Discordbot, Twitterbot) don't run JS, so anything React renders is invisible to them. The fix has to inject meta tags server-side before the HTML reaches the crawler. Receipts are publicly viewable, so this is straightforward.

## Approach

A Vercel serverless function intercepts `/receipts/:id` (and `/receipt/:id`), fetches a small public preview payload from the backend, and returns the SPA's `index.html` with OG/Twitter meta tags injected into `<head>`. All other routes continue to serve `index.html` unchanged via the existing rewrite. The same function path also fixes the static defaults so any non-receipt unfurl shows real Splitzy branding rather than the CRA placeholder.

This requires one new public backend endpoint that returns just the fields safe to expose in a preview (merchant, date, total) — keeping the OG function fast and avoiding leaking line items / assignments / user data.

## Files to change

### Backend

1. **`backend/blueprints/receipts.py`** — add a new public route:
   - `GET /api/receipts/<int:receipt_id>/preview`
   - No auth (matches "public link" model). Returns `{ merchant, date (ISO), total }` or 404.
   - Use existing `UserReceipt` model (`backend/models/user_receipt.py`); reuse the schema field selection pattern from `backend/schemas/receipt.py` rather than hand-serializing.
   - Set `Cache-Control: public, max-age=300, s-maxage=3600` so crawlers and Vercel both cache.
   - Soft-deleted receipts return 404 (check `deleted_at` if present).

2. **`backend/tests/test_receipts.py`** — add tests:
   - 200 with expected fields for an existing receipt.
   - 404 for unknown id and soft-deleted receipts.
   - No auth header required.

### Frontend / Vercel

3. **`frontend/api/og-receipt.ts`** *(new)* — Vercel serverless function:
   - Parse receipt id from `req.url` (handles both `/receipts/:id` and `/receipt/:id`; ignore trailing slashes / query strings).
   - `fetch('https://splitzy-prod.onrender.com/api/receipts/<id>/preview')` with a short timeout (~1.5s) and `AbortController`.
   - Read `frontend/dist/index.html` from the deployed bundle (Vercel includes it; use `fs.readFileSync` against `process.cwd()` or import via `@vercel/edge`'s asset support — node runtime is simpler here).
   - Inject the meta-tag block (template below) by replacing a sentinel placeholder we add to `index.html` (e.g. `<!--OG_TAGS-->`) — safer than regex on `<head>`.
   - On any failure (timeout, 404, fetch error) fall back to the default tag block so the page still loads with generic branding.
   - HTML-escape merchant text before injection.
   - Respond with `Content-Type: text/html` and a sensible `Cache-Control` (e.g. `public, s-maxage=3600, stale-while-revalidate=86400`).

4. **`frontend/vercel.json`** — add rewrites *before* the catch-all:
   ```json
   { "source": "/receipts/:id", "destination": "/api/og-receipt?id=:id" },
   { "source": "/receipt/:id",  "destination": "/api/og-receipt?id=:id" }
   ```
   Keep the existing `/api/:path*` proxy and `/(.*) → /index.html` fallback below these.

5. **`frontend/index.html`** — replace the CRA-era defaults and add the placeholder:
   - Replace `<title>Splitzy</title>` and the `description` meta with real defaults.
   - Add default OG + Twitter card tags (title, description, type=website, site_name, image).
   - Add `<!--OG_TAGS-->` sentinel inside `<head>` so the function can swap it.
   - Remove the stale CRA comment block.

## Meta-tag template (rendered by `og-receipt.ts`)

For a receipt with `merchant="Trader Joe's"`, `date="2026-04-12"`, `total=48.21`:

```html
<title>Trader Joe's — Apr 12, 2026 · Splitzy</title>
<meta name="description" content="Receipt total $48.21 · Split with friends on Splitzy" />

<meta property="og:type" content="website" />
<meta property="og:site_name" content="Splitzy" />
<meta property="og:title" content="Trader Joe's — Apr 12, 2026" />
<meta property="og:description" content="Receipt total $48.21 · Split with friends on Splitzy" />

<meta name="twitter:title" content="Trader Joe's — Apr 12, 2026" />
<meta name="twitter:description" content="Receipt total $48.21 · Split with friends on Splitzy" />
```

23	def upload_to_blob_storage(image_data, filename, content_type):
24	    """
25	    Upload binary image data to Vercel blob storage via the Vercel function
26	    Args:
27	        image_data (bytes): Binary image data
28	        filename (str): Original filename
29	        content_type (str): MIME type (e.g., 'image/jpeg')
30	    Returns the blob URL on success, None on failure
31	    """
32	    safe_filename = "unknown_file"  # Default for error logging
33

35	        # Get the Vercel function URL from environment
36	        # This is validated at app startup
37	        vercel_function_url = os.environ.get("VERCEL_FUNCTION_URL")

46	        safe_filename = secure_filename(filename) if filename else "uploaded_file"
47	        if (
48	            not safe_filename
49	        ):  # secure_filename might return empty string for invalid filenames
50	            safe_filename = "uploaded_file"
51	
52	        # Ensure content_type has a sensible default
53	        safe_content_type = content_type if content_type else "application/octet-stream"
54	
55	        # Prepare the file for upload using binary data
56	        files = {"file": (safe_filename, image_data, safe_content_type)}
57	
58	        # Make the request to the Vercel function
59	        response = requests.post(vercel_function_url, files=files, timeout=30)
60	
61	        # Raise HTTPError for bad HTTP status codes (4xx, 5xx)
62	        response.raise_for_status()
63	
64	        # Safe JSON parsing with error handling

68	            current_app.logger.exception(

72	            )
73	            return None
74	
75	        # Safe access to result data

77	            current_app.logger.error(
78	                f"Unexpected response format from blob storage: {type(result)}"
79	            )

from sqlalchemy.dialects.postgresql import JSONB

from models import db


class UserReceipt(db.Model):
    __tablename__ = "user_receipts"

    id = db.Column(
        db.BigInteger, primary_key=True
    )  # Using BigInteger for better scalability
    user_id = db.Column(db.BigInteger, db.ForeignKey("users.id"), nullable=True)
    image_path = db.Column(
        db.Text, nullable=True
    )  # Using Text for unlimited length URLs
    image_visibility = db.Column(
        db.Text, nullable=False, default="public"
    )  # 'public' | 'owner_only'
    created_at = db.Column(
        db.TIMESTAMP(timezone=True), server_default=text("CURRENT_TIMESTAMP")
    )
    deleted_at = db.Column(db.TIMESTAMP(timezone=True), nullable=True, index=True)

    # Denormalized fields extracted from receipt_data (RegularReceipt / TransportationTicket)
    is_receipt = db.Column(db.Boolean, nullable=True, default=True)
    document_type = db.Column(db.Text, nullable=True)

    # Regular receipt fields
    merchant = db.Column(db.Text, nullable=True)  # Using Text for unlimited length
    date = db.Column(db.Date, nullable=True)  # Using proper Date type
    subtotal = db.Column(Numeric(12, 2), nullable=True, default=0)
    tax = db.Column(Numeric(12, 2), nullable=True, default=0)
    original_tax = db.Column(Numeric(12, 2), nullable=True, default=0)
    tip = db.Column(Numeric(12, 2), nullable=True, default=0)
    original_tip = db.Column(Numeric(12, 2), nullable=True, default=0)
    gratuity = db.Column(Numeric(12, 2), nullable=True, default=0)
    total = db.Column(Numeric(12, 2), nullable=True, default=0)
    payment_method = db.Column(db.Text, nullable=True)
    tax_included_in_items = db.Column(db.Boolean, nullable=True, default=False)
    tip_after_tax = db.Column(db.Boolean, nullable=False, default=False)
    display_subtotal = db.Column(Numeric(12, 2), nullable=True, default=0)
    items_total = db.Column(Numeric(12, 2), nullable=True, default=0)
    pretax_total = db.Column(Numeric(12, 2), nullable=True, default=0)
    posttax_total = db.Column(Numeric(12, 2), nullable=True, default=0)
    final_total = db.Column(Numeric(12, 2), nullable=True, default=0)

    # Transportation ticket-specific fields
    carrier = db.Column(db.Text, nullable=True)  # Using Text for unlimited length
    ticket_number = db.Column(db.Text, nullable=True)  # Using Text for unlimited length
    origin = db.Column(db.Text, nullable=True)  # Using Text for unlimited length
    destination = db.Column(db.Text, nullable=True)  # Using Text for unlimited length
    passenger = db.Column(db.Text, nullable=True)  # Using Text for unlimited length
    class_ = db.Column("class", db.Text, nullable=True)
    fare = db.Column(Numeric(12, 2), nullable=True, default=0)
    currency = db.Column(db.Text, nullable=True)
    taxes = db.Column(Numeric(12, 2), nullable=True, default=0)

    # Additional metadata field for flexible data storage
    receipt_metadata = db.Column(
        JSONB, nullable=True, default=dict
    )  # Using JSONB for better performance and querying

    user = db.relationship("User", backref=db.backref("receipts", lazy=True))

    # Relationship to normalized line items
    line_items = db.relationship(
        "ReceiptLineItem",
        backref="receipt",
        lazy=True,
        cascade="all, delete-orphan",
        order_by="ReceiptLineItem.id",
    )

    def __repr__(self):
        return f"<UserReceipt {self.id}>"

import json
import os
from unittest.mock import patch

import pytest
from pydantic import ValidationError

from models import db
from schemas.receipt import BoundingBox, FieldMetadata, RegularReceipt


class TestBoundingBoxCoercion:
    """BoundingBox should accept both dict and [x1,y1,x2,y2] list forms."""

    def test_dict_form_accepted(self):
        bb = BoundingBox(x=10, y=20, width=100, height=50)
        assert bb.x == 10 and bb.y == 20 and bb.width == 100 and bb.height == 50

    def test_list_form_converted(self):
        """Gemini sometimes returns [x1, y1, x2, y2] corner coordinates."""
        bb = BoundingBox.model_validate([67, 70, 397, 135])
        assert bb.x == 67
        assert bb.y == 70
        assert bb.width == 330   # 397 - 67
        assert bb.height == 65   # 135 - 70

    def test_tuple_form_converted(self):
        bb = BoundingBox.model_validate((10, 5, 15, 30))
        assert bb.x == 10 and bb.y == 5
        assert bb.width == 5 and bb.height == 25  # corner coercion: 15-10, 30-5

    def test_ambiguous_list_treated_as_origin_size(self):
        """When values don't unambiguously represent corners, treat as [x, y, w, h]."""
        bb = BoundingBox.model_validate([100, 100, 100, 100])
        assert bb.x == 100 and bb.y == 100
        assert bb.width == 100 and bb.height == 100

    def test_origin_size_list_not_corrupted(self):
        """A list already in [x, y, width, height] where w < x should pass through."""
        bb = BoundingBox.model_validate([200, 300, 80, 20])
        assert bb.x == 200 and bb.y == 300
        assert bb.width == 80 and bb.height == 20

    def test_list_wrong_length_raises(self):
        with pytest.raises(ValidationError):
            BoundingBox.model_validate([10, 20, 30])

    def test_field_metadata_with_list_bbox(self):
        """End-to-end: FieldMetadata should validate when bbox is a list."""
        entry = {
            "field_name": "merchant",
            "bbox": [67, 70, 397, 135],
            "is_pii": False,
            "pii_category": None,
        }
        fm = FieldMetadata.model_validate(entry)
        assert fm.bbox.x == 67
        assert fm.bbox.y == 70
        assert fm.bbox.width == 330   # 397 - 67
        assert fm.bbox.height == 65   # 135 - 70


def test_health_check(test_client):
    """
    GIVEN a Flask application
    WHEN the '/api/health' page is requested
    THEN check that a '200' status code is returned
    """
    response = test_client.get("/api/health")
    assert response.status_code == 200
    data = json.loads(response.data)
    assert data["status"] == "healthy"


@patch("blueprints.receipts.upload_to_blob_storage")
@patch("blueprints.receipts.ImageAnalyzer")
def test_analyze_receipt(
    mock_image_analyzer, mock_blob_upload, test_client, new_user, mock_receipt_data
):
    """
    GIVEN a Flask application
    WHEN the '/api/analyze-receipt' page is sent a POST request with a file
    THEN check that a '200' status code is returned and the receipt data is in the response
    """
    # Log in the user
    with test_client.session_transaction() as session:
        session["user_id"] = new_user.id

    # Mock the blob storage upload to return a fake URL
    mock_blob_upload.return_value = "https://fake-blob-storage.com/fake-image-url.jpg"

    # Mock the ImageAnalyzer result
    mock_analyzer_instance = mock_image_analyzer.return_value
    mock_analyzer_instance.analyze_image.return_value = RegularReceipt.model_validate(
        mock_receipt_data
    )

    # Create a dummy file to upload
    upload_folder = test_client.application.config.get("UPLOAD_FOLDER", "/tmp")
    if not os.path.exists(upload_folder):
        os.makedirs(upload_folder)
    file_path = os.path.join(upload_folder, "receipt.jpg")
    with open(file_path, "w") as f:
        f.write("dummy receipt image")

    with open(file_path, "rb") as f:
        response = test_client.post(
            "/api/analyze-receipt",
            data={"file": (f, "receipt.jpg")},
            content_type="multipart/form-data",
        )

    assert response.status_code == 200
    data = json.loads(response.data)
    assert data["success"] is True
    assert data["is_receipt"] is True
    assert data["receipt_data"]["merchant"] == mock_receipt_data["merchant"]

    os.remove(file_path)

import json
import os
from unittest.mock import patch

import pytest
from pydantic import ValidationError

from models import db
from schemas.receipt import BoundingBox, FieldMetadata, RegularReceipt


class TestBoundingBoxCoercion:
    """BoundingBox should accept both dict and [x1,y1,x2,y2] list forms."""

    def test_dict_form_accepted(self):
        bb = BoundingBox(x=10, y=20, width=100, height=50)
        assert bb.x == 10 and bb.y == 20 and bb.width == 100 and bb.height == 50

    def test_list_form_converted(self):
        """Gemini sometimes returns [x1, y1, x2, y2] corner coordinates."""
        bb = BoundingBox.model_validate([67, 70, 397, 135])
        assert bb.x == 67
        assert bb.y == 70
        assert bb.width == 330   # 397 - 67
        assert bb.height == 65   # 135 - 70

    def test_tuple_form_converted(self):
        bb = BoundingBox.model_validate((10, 5, 15, 30))
        assert bb.x == 10 and bb.y == 5
        assert bb.width == 5 and bb.height == 25  # corner coercion: 15-10, 30-5

    def test_ambiguous_list_treated_as_origin_size(self):
        """When values don't unambiguously represent corners, treat as [x, y, w, h]."""
        bb = BoundingBox.model_validate([100, 100, 100, 100])
        assert bb.x == 100 and bb.y == 100
        assert bb.width == 100 and bb.height == 100

    def test_origin_size_list_not_corrupted(self):
        """A list already in [x, y, width, height] where w < x should pass through."""
        bb = BoundingBox.model_validate([200, 300, 80, 20])
        assert bb.x == 200 and bb.y == 300
        assert bb.width == 80 and bb.height == 20

    def test_list_wrong_length_raises(self):
        with pytest.raises(ValidationError):
            BoundingBox.model_validate([10, 20, 30])

    def test_field_metadata_with_list_bbox(self):
        """End-to-end: FieldMetadata should validate when bbox is a list."""
        entry = {
            "field_name": "merchant",
            "bbox": [67, 70, 397, 135],
            "is_pii": False,
            "pii_category": None,
        }
        fm = FieldMetadata.model_validate(entry)
        assert fm.bbox.x == 67
        assert fm.bbox.y == 70
        assert fm.bbox.width == 330   # 397 - 67
        assert fm.bbox.height == 65   # 135 - 70

class TestReceiptPreview:
    """GET /api/receipts/<id>/preview — public endpoint for OG link previews."""

    def _make_receipt(self, **overrides):
        defaults = dict(
            merchant="Trader Joe's",
            date=date(2026, 4, 12),
            total=Decimal("48.21"),
        )
        defaults.update(overrides)
        receipt = UserReceipt(**defaults)
        db.session.add(receipt)
        db.session.commit()
        return receipt

    def test_returns_merchant_date_total(self, test_app, test_client):
        with test_app.app_context():
            receipt = self._make_receipt()
            response = test_client.get(f"/api/receipts/{receipt.id}/preview")

        assert response.status_code == 200
        data = json.loads(response.data)
        assert data == {
            "merchant": "Trader Joe's",
            "date": "2026-04-12",
            "total": 48.21,
        }
        assert "public" in response.headers.get("Cache-Control", "")

    def test_no_auth_required(self, test_app, test_client):
        with test_app.app_context():
            receipt = self._make_receipt()
            response = test_client.get(f"/api/receipts/{receipt.id}/preview")

        assert response.status_code == 200

    def test_unknown_id_returns_404(self, test_client):
        response = test_client.get("/api/receipts/999999/preview")
        assert response.status_code == 404

    def test_soft_deleted_returns_404(self, test_app, test_client):
        with test_app.app_context():
            receipt = self._make_receipt(
                deleted_at=datetime.now(timezone.utc)
            )
            response = test_client.get(f"/api/receipts/{receipt.id}/preview")

        assert response.status_code == 404

    def test_null_fields_serialized_as_null(self, test_app, test_client):
        with test_app.app_context():
            receipt = self._make_receipt(
                merchant=None, date=None, total=None
            )
            response = test_client.get(f"/api/receipts/{receipt.id}/preview")

        assert response.status_code == 200
        data = json.loads(response.data)
        assert data == {"merchant": None, "date": None, "total": None}

        os.makedirs(upload_folder)
    file_path = os.path.join(upload_folder, "receipt.jpg")
    with open(file_path, "w") as f:
        f.write("dummy receipt image")

    with open(file_path, "rb") as f:
        response = test_client.post(
            "/api/analyze-receipt",
            data={"file": (f, "receipt.jpg")},
            content_type="multipart/form-data",
        )

    assert response.status_code == 200
    data = json.loads(response.data)
    assert data["success"] is True
    assert data["is_receipt"] is True
    assert data["receipt_data"]["merchant"] == mock_receipt_data["merchant"]

    os.remove(file_path)


class TestReceiptPreview:
    """GET /api/receipts/<id>/preview — public endpoint for OG link previews."""

    def _make_receipt(self, **overrides):
        defaults = dict(
            merchant="Trader Joe's",
            date=date(2026, 4, 12),
            total=Decimal("48.21"),
        )
        defaults.update(overrides)
        receipt = UserReceipt(**defaults)
        db.session.add(receipt)
        db.session.commit()
        return receipt

    def test_returns_merchant_date_total(self, test_app, test_client):
        with test_app.app_context():
            receipt = self._make_receipt()
            response = test_client.get(f"/api/receipts/{receipt.id}/preview")

        assert response.status_code == 200
        data = json.loads(response.data)
        assert data == {
            "merchant": "Trader Joe's",
            "date": "2026-04-12",
            "total": 48.21,
        }
        assert "public" in response.headers.get("Cache-Control", "")

    def test_no_auth_required(self, test_app, test_client):
        with test_app.app_context():
            receipt = self._make_receipt()
            response = test_client.get(f"/api/receipts/{receipt.id}/preview")

        assert response.status_code == 200

    def test_unknown_id_returns_404(self, test_client):
        response = test_client.get("/api/receipts/999999/preview")
        assert response.status_code == 404

    def test_soft_deleted_returns_404(self, test_app, test_client):
        with test_app.app_context():
            receipt = self._make_receipt(
                deleted_at=datetime.now(timezone.utc)
            )
            response = test_client.get(f"/api/receipts/{receipt.id}/preview")

        assert response.status_code == 404

    def test_null_fields_serialized_as_null(self, test_app, test_client):
        with test_app.app_context():
            receipt = self._make_receipt(
                merchant=None, date=None, total=None
            )
            response = test_client.get(f"/api/receipts/{receipt.id}/preview")

        assert response.status_code == 200
        data = json.loads(response.data)
        assert data == {"merchant": None, "date": None, "total": None}

**Backend** (`backend/blueprints/receipts.py` + `backend/tests/test_receipts.py`)
- New public `GET /api/receipts/<id>/preview` returning `{merchant, date, total}` with 5-min browser / 1-hr CDN cache.
- Soft-deleted receipts → 404. Tests cover 200 path, 404s, no-auth access, and null fields.

**Frontend / Vercel**
- `frontend/api/og-receipt.js` — Vercel serverless function that fetches the preview, escapes the merchant text, formats the date and total, and injects OG/Twitter tags between `<!--OG_TAGS-->` markers in `dist/index.html`. Falls back to defaults on any fetch failure (timeout 1.5s, non-200, malformed id).
- `frontend/vercel.json` — added rewrites for `/receipts/:id` and `/receipt/:id` to the new function, plus `includeFiles` so `dist/index.html` is bundled with it.
- `frontend/index.html` — replaced the CRA placeholder with real Splitzy defaults (title, description, OG, Twitter card) wrapped in the `<!--OG_TAGS-->...<!--/OG_TAGS-->` sentinel.

**Verified**
- Backend preview tests pass (5/5).
- Local smoke test of the function confirms three branches render correctly: full data → "Trader Joe's — Apr 12, 2026" + total in description; missing merchant → "Receipt — Apr 12, 2026"; backend unreachable → default Splitzy tags.

**To verify after deploy**
- `curl https://splitzy-prod.onrender.com/api/receipts/80/preview` → expected JSON.
- Paste a production `/receipts/80` URL into Slack/Discord and confirm the unfurled card.

message.content: do i need to do anything for the vercel function to be deployed?

content.text:
Short answer: **no extra step beyond a normal git push** — Vercel auto-deploys functions in `frontend/api/*.js`. But there are a few things worth checking.

**What deploys automatically**
- Any file in `frontend/api/` is detected as a serverless function by Vercel.
- `vercel.json` rewrites take effect on the next deploy.
- `includeFiles: "dist/index.html"` is honored as long as the build (`pnpm build`) runs before the function is bundled — which it does on Vercel because `framework: "vite"` runs the build first.

**Things to confirm / do**

1. **Push to the branch your Vercel project tracks** (probably `main`). The deploy kicks off automatically. Preview deploys also work for PRs.

2. **No new env vars are required.** The function defaults `BACKEND_URL` to `https://splitzy-prod.onrender.com`, matching your existing API rewrite. If you ever want to override it (e.g. for staging), set `BACKEND_URL` in the Vercel project's env vars.

3. **Backend has to ship first (or at least at the same time).** The Vercel function calls `splitzy-prod.onrender.com/api/receipts/:id/preview` — which only exists once the backend changes are merged and Render redeploys. If Vercel ships first, receipt URLs will just fall back to the default Splitzy preview (graceful), but you won't see merchant/date until Render is up.

4. **Verify after deploy:**
   - Hit the backend directly: `curl https://splitzy-prod.onrender.com/api/receipts/80/preview` → expect JSON.
   - Hit the function: `curl -s https://<your-prod-domain>/receipts/80 | grep og:title` → expect the merchant + date.
   - Use Slack's link unfurl debugger or `https://www.opengraph.xyz/url/...` to confirm the card.

5. **Cache caveat.** Slack/Discord cache previews aggressively. After deploy, old links may keep showing the CRA description for a while. Slack: re-share or use `/debug-link <url>` to refresh. Discord: append `?v=1` to bust.

6. **Optional but recommended:** test on a Vercel preview deployment (PR branch) before merging to `main`, since this is the first function that intercepts a user-visible route.

Feat: per-receipt Open Graph link previews

Replace the leftover create-react-app meta tags so /receipts/:id
links unfurl with merchant, date, and total in Slack/Discord/etc.

- Backend: public GET /api/receipts/<id>/preview returning
  {merchant, date, total} with CDN-friendly cache headers.
- Vercel: serverless function (frontend/api/og-receipt.js) that
  intercepts /receipts/:id and /receipt/:id, fetches the preview,
  and injects OG/Twitter tags into index.html between sentinel
  comments. Falls back to default tags on any failure.
- index.html: real Splitzy defaults plus the OG_TAGS sentinel.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
EOF
)" && git log -1 --stat
